title: The figure on paper: fold it and carry it for thirty days
subtitle: A folk-ritual practice for making the wealth number you're afraid to claim into an object you carry.
excerpt: A spell practice from Day 24 of the MONEY program. Write the figure that scares you, fold the paper, and carry it with you for thirty days. Use to move from the number being abstract and alarming to being familiar and ordinary.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education, drawing on the folk-magic tradition of written intentions carried on the body. Aligned with Day 24 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to normalise a specific wealth figure by keeping it physically close until it stops feeling impossible.

content.text: A folk practice from Day 24 of the MONEY program. The premise is simple: the number you can't write down without a shiver of impossibility is the number to carry. Familiarity is the medicine. Thirty days of the paper in your pocket or bra. No chanting, no ceremony, just contact.
Somewhere to carry it on your body for thirty days (a pocket, a bra, a purse)
Sit quietly. Ask yourself: what is the figure that, when I write it down, makes me feel like it could not possibly be mine?
Write it on the piece of paper. No currency symbol, no commas. Just the digits.
Carry it on your body for the next thirty days. You don't need to look at it. Just keep it with you.
On day thirty, unfold it and read it again. Write one sentence about how the number feels different now.
After thirty days, you can burn it, bury it, or keep it. The choice is yours.
Original to homemade.education, drawing on the folk-magic tradition of carrying written intentions close to the body. Aligned with Day 24 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
